arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line objdump readelf 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'llvm|binutils' | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try Microsoft's symbol server for the debug symbols
# BuildId: 4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"

# Try debuginfo endpoint  
echo "=== Trying debuginfo ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo ""

# Try the binary itself
echo "=== Trying binary ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so"
echo ""

# Try NuGet symbol server
echo "=== Trying NuGet symbol server ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo ""

curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so"
echo ""
arguments.description: Try downloading symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Try downloading symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The tombstone shows a crash in a .NET Android app (CoreCLR-based). The key frames are in `libcoreclr.so` with BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`. I need to symbolicate frames #01-#06.

To symbolicate these, I need the debug symbols for `libcoreclr.so` with that specific BuildId. The BuildId is `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`.


Microsoft publishes symbol packages for .NET runtime libraries on their symbol servers, so I can try downloading the debug symbols from there and then use tools like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to resolve the addresses. The Microsoft symbol server supports both traditional and debuginfod-style endpoints for ELF files.

I have the BuildId from the tombstone (`4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`), so I can construct the proper URL to fetch the symbol file—either through the standard symbol server path or the debuginfod protocol endpoint. I'm constructing the full URL to download the symbol file from Microsoft's server, then I'll check what debugging tools are available locally and attempt to resolve the addresses using addr2line or llvm-symbolizer. Since this is a CoreCLR crash with a specific BuildId, I should verify I can actually handle a large library download before proceeding.
